data.title: MISTRAS Group Issues Clarification Regarding Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items (Non-GAAP) Referenced During Q4 2025 Earnings Call
data.description: In Princeton Junction, New Jersey, MISTRAS Group, Inc. (NYSE: MG), a prominent provider of technology-driven industrial asset integrity and lab testing solutions, has amended its earlier statement on Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items (Non-GAAP) from the Q4 2025 earnings conference call held on March 5, 2026. It was initially mentioned that the company's Diluted EPS Excluding Special Items for the three months ending December 31, 2025, was $0.20 per share. However, the accurate figure for the mentioned period stood at $0.25 per share, as officially documented in MISTRAS Group's earnings press release, SEC filings, and earnings presentation. This clarification stresses the corrected value and ensures precision in financial reporting for stakeholders and interested parties.

data.title: Totem Evolves the Fastest-Growing Offline Navigation Device with Version 4.1 and Redesigned App Experience
data.description: A company based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, revealed the launch of version 4.1 of their innovative peer-to-peer navigation device, Totem Compass, which operates without cellular service. Totem, recognized for its groundbreaking technology, also released a revamped Totem Compass App alongside the device upgrade. This marks a substantial leap in performance and user-friendliness since the product's introduction to the market in 2024. The latest version promises notable enhancements to the Totem Compass, establishing a significant technological advancement for the brand.

data.title: BJ's Wholesale Club CEO Flags 'Dynamic Environment' Of Tariffs, Cautious Consumers
data.description: BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: BJ) saw a drop in its shares despite surpassing expectations in both earnings and revenue for the fourth quarter. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 96 cents, beating the predicted 92 cents. Quarterly sales reached $5.575 billion, a 5.6% increase compared to the estimated $5.539 billion. Comparable club sales rose by 1.6% year over year, or 2.6% excluding gasoline sales. Gross profit went up to $1.01 billion from $949.0 million. Operating income slightly decreased by 0.2% to $178.078 million. Adjusted EBITDA showed a 0.7% increase, reaching $266.497 million. Membership fee income surged by 10.9% year over year. However, the company's fiscal 2026 outlook fell short of Wall Street's expectations, leading to the decline in share value.
